title: The RS Bistable Circuit
summary: Analyses the no-clock RS bistable circuit using Karnaugh diagrams to identify stable states and explains its set/reset/hold/forbidden behaviour.
content_points: Two NOR gates with cross-coupled feedback; two inputs (R, S), two outputs (Q, P), with P = NOT Q.
Stable state analysis via Karnaugh: (Q,P) stabilises when (Q,P) = (q,p).
(R=0,S=1): always stabilises at Q=1 (set). (R=1,S=0): always Q=0 (reset).
(R=0,S=0): holds current state (P≠Q). (R=1,S=1): Q=P=0 – forbidden (P=Q violates bit semantics).
learning_objectives: Analyse the RS bistable using stable-state Karnaugh analysis
Describe the set, reset, hold, and forbidden states
